free_text: New measurement of excitation functions for (p,x)
reactions on natMo with  special regard to the
formation of 95mTc, 96m+gTc, 99mTc and 99Mo
cyclotron U-120M in the Institute of
Nuclear Physics AS CR
Beam energy was calculated
from precise measurement of the beam orbit and
stopping power of protons in the stack and compared
with the energy value based on the activity ratio of
two simultaneously produced zinc isotopes in the
copper monitors. Beam current was calculated from
the activity of one of the produced zinc isotopes
and the activation formula and compared with the
value based on the total beam charge deposited on the
target, which was measured by a calibrated beam
current integrator (in the case of the first stack,
the beam current was 0.975 mu-A, in the case of the
second stack, 1.050 mu-A, and in the case of the
third stack 0.810 mu-A).
Three stacks were irradiated in order to minimize
the effect of beam energy dispersion. 20.0 mu-m thick
Mo foils were used. For beam monitoring 10.6 mu-m
thick Cu  foils and as a beam energy degrader one or
two 55 mu-m thick Cu  foils, exceptionally one
23.7 mu-m Cu foil. In the third stack, two 14.4 mu-m
Ni foils were inserted  as further beam monitors. Each
stack was finished by as many  250 mu-m Ag foils as
necessary for total beam stop that were directly
cooled from the reverse side by water.
Gamma-spectrometer equipped with an HPGe
detector. The spectrometer was calibrated by a set
of  standards (241Am, 133Ba, 152Eu, 137Cs, 60Co)
supplied by the Czech  Institute for Metrology with
combined standard uncertainties  varying between 0.4%
Overall uncertainty of each cross-section is
given by  combining the following single errors in
quadrature:
uncertainty of the beam current
measurements(8%);
uncertainty of the absolute activities
(usually 4-15%);
uncertainty of the Mo foils
thickness(<1%);
other uncertainties, i.e. foil
density, irradiation times, decay constants (<3%).
Data from tbl.4,5 from
SD
On. Authors' given name abbreviated.
cross-sections from cumulative activity of 93m+gTc
cross-sections from cumulative activity of 96m+gTc
cross-sections from cumulative activity of 90m+gNb
VS T1/2 corrected.
